calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN WONG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE the August 11, 2016 default and the April 4, 2017 default judgment is GRANTED and plaintiff Ibarra Professional Law Corporation's request for attorney's fees and costs is DENIED. While not the strongest case of extrinsic mistake, Mr. Wong has adequately shown all of the required elements to invoke the court's exercise of its discretion to relieve him of the default and default judgment on the grounds of extrinsic mistake. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff has not shown that it will suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Plaintiff's service of documents on Mr. Wong at an address that it had reason to know was neither Mr. Wong's business or home address, its seeking a huge amount of punitive damages for claims that are essentially contractual in nature against a former law firm client, and its arguable non-compliance with Business and Professions Code 6201(a) are additional reasons why the court exercises its discretion to grant the motion and deny plaintiff's request for what appears to be a disproportionately large amount of fees and costs.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED

Notice Of Hearing Of Default Prove-Up
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy, Clerk: J. Bertelsen, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.

Andrew Safont, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Ibarra Professional Law Corporation.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Stephen Y. Wong, American Media Enterprise Corp. Radio TV, and New World Commerce LLC.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.
Witness Cris Ibarra sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.
Court ordered clerk to file Proof of Service of Notice of Client's Right to Fee Arbitration as to Stephen Y. Wong and New World Commerce LLC.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:
Plaintiff shall recover against American Media and Wong, jointly and severally, $21,684.10 in principal, with interest thereon in the amount of $2,521.65;
Plaintiff shall recover against New World and Wong, jointly and severally, $89,902.04;
Plaintiff is awarded punitive damages against Wong in the amount of $334,758.42; and
Plaintiff is awarded costs in the amount of $515.20.

Judgment signed in open court.
